summary: Wave 1 already fixed the advisor observability boundary: stdout stays reserved for hook output, stderr carries redacted diagnostics, metrics track runtime/status/freshness/error/duration/retries/cache hits, and health belongs in a dedicated advisor surface or session_health section.

The checked-in hook utilities and Copilot session-prime surface already separate stdout from stderr, but their current INFO/WARN/ERROR text lines are human-readable diagnostics rather than fixed-key per-invocation telemetry.

The local advisor health surface already exposes status, cache health, source metadata health, topology warnings, and inventory parity drift, while the runtime cache exposes hit-relevant state such as cached records, skipped files, and signature-based invalidation.

The existing benchmark harness already gates warm p95 at 20 ms, cold p95 at 60 ms, and batch throughput at 2x, while the regression harness already tracks top1_accuracy, command_bridge_fp_rate, and p0_pass_rate; these become the background quality baseline for advisor observability.

The checked-in session_health handler already publishes status, structured sections, and actionable hints, so advisor status should land as a new advisor-hook-health peer section rather than as startup-banner text or a separate operator tool.

Use the shared metric namespace speckit_advisor_hook_* with fixed low-cardinality dimensions for runtime, hook_event, status, freshness, error_code, cache_layer, and cache_result so all runtimes can share one dashboard and alarm model.
Emit exactly one canonical JSONL record to stderr per advisor-hook invocation, using hashed identifiers and allowlisted fixed keys, while preserving stdout exclusively for model-visible hook output.

Adopt packet thresholds as first-class alarms: fail-open rate >1 percent over 1 hour, hook duration p99 >500 ms over 15 minutes, and inventory-cache hit rate <30 percent over 30 minutes, with additional alerts for degraded health, no recent success, inventory parity drift, and skipped SKILL files.

Add advisor-hook-health as a fifth session_health section with ok/warning/stale mapping based on advisor health status, fail-open rate, p99 latency, cache-hit rate, and age of last successful invocation, then reuse the existing hint model for operator guidance.

Logging raw prompts, normalized prompts, or advisor reason strings into metrics or structured logs remains ruled out because the wave-1 privacy contract and the future whitelist-only brief boundary both prohibit user-derived text from becoming an observability payload.
